skip_policy.description: checker_id with no registered Python implementation produces SKIP, never FAIL or exception. Absence of impl != business failure.

merge_policy.description: Module delta (00_*_MODULE_RULES.json) may only extend waiver policy via module_waiver_policy.additional_cannot_waive. Sovereign fields listed above are immutable by module delta.

global_type_system.note: TYPE-001..006 rules migrated to rule_sets.type_rules. This section retains type mapping reference data only.

document_shape_rules.assertion: Every required document must expose canonical metadata fields when applicable.
No promoted contract document may contain TODO, TBD, FIXME or placeholder markers.
12_ATLETAS_EXECUTION_BINDINGS.yaml must strictly validate against its canonical JSON Schema before handoff generation.
document_shape_rules.failure_message: Execution bindings document does not validate against canonical schema.

document_shape_rules.assertion: Every generated document must exist after planning and satisfy its minimum integrity contract. 16_ATLETAS_AGENT_HANDOFF.json must contain a valid self-referential zero-padded snapshot hash procedure and anchor_manifest.json must exist and be non-empty.
document_shape_rules.failure_message: Generated document missing or integrity contract invalid after planning.

cross_rules.assertion: Every operationId in OpenAPI must exist exactly once in traceability.operations.operation_id.
cross_rules.failure_message: Orphan operationId detected.

cross_rules.assertion: Every traceability operation_id must exist in OpenAPI.
cross_rules.failure_message: Traceability references non-existent contract operation.

cross_rules.assertion: Every write operation must declare required persistence bindings and required migrations.
cross_rules.failure_message: Write operation without DB contract binding.

cross_rules.assertion: Non-nullable persisted fields required for create/update must be representable in request contracts or explicitly defaulted by DB/service.
cross_rules.failure_message: DB nullability and API request contract mismatch.

cross_rules.assertion: Every UI-bound input field must bind to an OpenAPI request property or explicit derived field declaration.
cross_rules.failure_message: UI selector references unknown contract field.

cross_rules.assertion: Every declared required selector must be referenced by at least one bound screen or operation path.
cross_rules.failure_message: Declared selector is not traceable to execution.

cross_rules.assertion: Every referenced invariant_id must exist and have formal_predicate plus executable_pseudocode.
cross_rules.failure_message: Traceability references incomplete invariant.

cross_rules.assertion: Every hard_fail invariant must bind to at least one operation and one automated test.
cross_rules.failure_message: Hard-fail invariant lacks enforceable execution path.

cross_rules.assertion: Every symbol in file_map.symbol_bindings must be referenced by at least one traceability implementation_binding or invariant/reference function.
cross_rules.failure_message: Unreachable bound symbol.

cross_rules.assertion: Every table used in concurrent write paths must declare locking_policy.
cross_rules.failure_message: Concurrent write path without locking strategy.

cross_rules.assertion: Every screen state transition must define button enabled/disabled policy for submit-like actions.
cross_rules.failure_message: UI state machine incomplete for interactive action.

cross_rules.assertion: Handoff manifest hashes must match current contract files before executor start.
cross_rules.failure_message: Executor received stale or divergent handoff snapshot.

cross_rules.assertion: Executor task list must reference only traceability-declared operation_ids and file paths.
cross_rules.failure_message: Handoff contains non-contracted implementation target.

cross_rules.assertion: All generated frontend API types must derive from current OpenAPI snapshot.
cross_rules.failure_message: Frontend types stale relative to contract.

cross_rules.assertion: Required migrations in DB contract must exist and be ordered before implementation handoff.
cross_rules.failure_message: Missing or unordered migration dependency.

cross_rules.assertion: Every executable step, bound symbol, target path or operational binding declared in execution_bindings must have a corresponding traceability entry.
cross_rules.failure_message: Execution binding contains non-traceable executable step.

cross_rules.assertion: Execution bindings must not override, relax, bypass or replace constitutional prohibitions, fail-closed rules, scope restrictions or validator requirements.
cross_rules.failure_message: Execution binding attempts to override constitutional restriction.

cross_rules.assertion: The prohibited_keys array in 12_ATLETAS_EXECUTION_BINDINGS.yaml must be a complete superset of the constitutionally required prohibited execution overrides.
cross_rules.failure_message: Execution bindings prohibited_keys are incomplete relative to constitutional restriction map.

cross_rules.assertion: Execution flags in execution_bindings must be logically consistent with the structural role, implementation binding and protection intent declared in traceability for their target files and symbols.
cross_rules.failure_message: Execution flags are inconsistent with traceability-declared protection intent.

cross_rules.assertion: Implementation must satisfy all canonical scenarios declared in 19_ATLETAS_TEST_SCENARIOS.yaml, verified through deterministic test report artifacts with stable hashes and scenario-level pass/fail mapping.
cross_rules.failure_message: Canonical deterministic test scenarios did not pass or deterministic report is missing/invalid.

event_rules.assertion: Every event_type consumed by a projection must exist in AsyncAPI messages or x-hbtrack event catalog.
Every source event version used by projection must be explicitly supported or upcasted.
Every event-triggered side effect must reference a declared event_type and event_version.
event_rules.failure_message: Side effect bound to undeclared event.

event_rules.assertion: Projection handlers must not overlap with side-effect handlers for the same execution path.
event_rules.failure_message: Projection and side effect mixed in same handler path.

event_rules.assertion: If source_of_truth is event_store, every read model table must have at least one event mapping or explicit derived function mapping.
event_rules.failure_message: Read model field or table without event provenance.

event_rules.assertion: Event envelope aggregate_id type must match canonical aggregate identifier type in DB contract.
event_rules.failure_message: Aggregate ID type drift between event and persistence.

event_rules.assertion: Event and projection contracts must agree on aggregate_id as partition key.
event_rules.failure_message: Partition key drift detected between event and projection contracts.

event_rules.assertion: Upcasters must be pure functions over validated event input and constant injected defaults only.
Pydantic model_construct is forbidden in event schemas, upcasters and projection pipeline.
event_rules.failure_message: Forbidden model_construct usage detected in event pipeline.

projection_rules.assertion: No read-model field may exist without source event mapping or explicit derived function.
No write to target projection table may bypass declared event handlers.
Every event version consumed by a projection must be explicitly listed.
If a new event version is introduced, replay compatibility must be declared by support list or upcaster.
Projection rebuild must be replay-safe and side-effect-free.
Projection handlers must preserve transaction_scope, idempotency guard and ledger mark shell.
Projection handlers must not open nested or manual transactions inside executor-controlled regions.
Projection-owned tables must not be written by direct app flows outside declared projection handlers.
Projection contract must declare idempotency ledger and uniqueness key for applied events.

Accumulator fields in read models must declare sequencing dependency and deterministic fold strategy for out-of-order replay. Any sum, average, count, rolling metric or stateful aggregate field must specify ordering key, accumulation semantics and replay consistency policy.
projection_rules.failure_message: Accumulator field lacks deterministic sequencing contract.

side_effect_rules.assertion: Every external side effect must declare idempotency_key derived from unique event inputs.
Every external side effect must declare replay_policy.
Every external side effect must declare error_policy and retry policy when retryable.
Projection rebuild paths must skip side effects unless replay_policy explicitly allows execution.
No undeclared network call or integration symbol may appear outside 18_ATLETAS_SIDE_EFFECTS.yaml or explicitly allowed adapters.
Side-effect handlers must not mutate projection tables or domain tables.
Side-effect handlers must not import projection modules or projection infrastructure.
Side-effect handlers must not use system clock as business input.
Side-effect handlers must use only integration symbols declared in the side-effect contract.
Side-effect handlers must preserve SideEffectResult wrapper contract and result logging shell.
Generated trigger logic for side effects must be semantically equivalent to the declared predicate in 18_ATLETAS_SIDE_EFFECTS.yaml.
Every side-effect consumer with trigger_condition must have a normalized predicate entry recorded in generated manifest artifacts.
side_effect_rules.failure_message: Missing predicate manifest entry for side-effect trigger.

concurrency_rules.assertion: Every aggregate with concurrent update risk must declare one of: optimistic_locking, pessimistic_locking, append_only_eventing.
Optimistic locking requires version_column plus conflict_error_code plus retry_policy.
Interactive submit flows for concurrent writes must define double_click protection or equivalent client-side duplicate prevention.
concurrency_rules.failure_message: UI duplicate submit protection missing for concurrent write path.

ui_state_rules.assertion: Every submitting state must define submit control policy.
Every success/error state must define visible feedback selector.
Every required selector must use data-testid as primary automation surface when ui_contract.selector_strategy.primary=data-testid.

time_determinism_rules.assertion: Every invariant depending on temporal context must declare explicit required_inputs and canonical reference date or reference year.
time_determinism_rules.failure_message: Temporal invariant without deterministic time input.

time_determinism_rules.assertion: datetime.now, date.today and system clock usage are forbidden in invariant enforcement paths unless explicitly contract-approved.
Frozen time must be enabled for temporal scenarios marked deterministic_time_required.
time_determinism_rules.failure_message: Temporal scenario executed without frozen time.

test_scenario_rules.assertion: Domain validation tests must use canonical scenario_ids from 19_ATLETAS_TEST_SCENARIOS.yaml.
New domain scenario requires contract update before execution.
Property-based and fuzzing tests may be used for schema robustness only and must not redefine domain truth.

stub_anchor_rules.assertion: Executor may edit only within generated anchor regions HB-BODY-START/HB-BODY-END or UI equivalent markers.
Generated symbol names, signatures, decorators and file paths are immutable unless regenerated by hb_plan.py.
Executor must not create new public symbols outside traceability-declared symbols.
Every anchored file must include contract hash comment matching current handoff snapshot.

handoff_rules.assertion: Executor can start only from structured manifest with integrity hashes.
If any required artifact hash differs from snapshot, execution must be blocked.
Executor task plan must be a subset of allowed operations and allowed file paths.

restriction_prompt_rules.assertion: Executor restriction prompt must declare fail_closed mode.
Executor restriction prompt must explicitly forbid using chat history as source of truth.
Executor restriction prompt must explicitly require STOP and BLOCKED_INPUT behavior on missing or inconsistent contracts.

Executor restriction prompt text must explicitly contain the constitutional prohibitions and fail-closed mandates declared by this constitution, including prohibition of chat-history-as-truth, prohibition of out-of-contract implementation, mandatory blocked-input behavior on contract gaps, and authority of structured manifests over narrative instructions.
restriction_prompt_rules.failure_message: Executor restriction prompt text is not constitutionally compliant.

diff_validation_rules.assertion: No structural diff outside anchored regions may be accepted.
Generated files regenerated by hb_plan.py must match expected template checksum except for anchored body regions.

type_rules.assertion: Every OpenAPI scalar must map to a canonical backend/frontend/db tuple.

Fields declared as canonical UUID must preserve uuid semantics across contracts: OpenAPI string+format=uuid, DB uuid/native equivalent, backend UUID/native equivalent, frontend string constrained by contract format uuid.
type_rules.failure_message: UUID canonical format drift detected across layers.

type_rules.assertion: Frontend generated types must derive from the current OpenAPI snapshot, never from handwritten approximation.

Enum member identity must be preserved across all contract layers. For every canonical enum, member names and values must match exactly across OpenAPI, AsyncAPI/events, DB contract, backend generated types and any declared UI/domain contract usage.
type_rules.failure_message: Enum member identity drift detected across contract layers.

type_rules.assertion: Collection element types must maintain canonical mapping consistency across all layers. Arrays/lists/JSON collections must preserve element identity, ordering policy, nullability policy and canonical scalar/enum semantics across OpenAPI, events, backend types and persistence contracts.
type_rules.failure_message: Collection element type drift detected across layers.

type_rules.assertion: Polymorphic schemas using oneOf or anyOf must declare and preserve a canonical discriminator strategy across OpenAPI, backend type system, event contracts and persistence mapping. Discriminator field, discriminator values and inheritance strategy must be explicit and consistent.
type_rules.failure_message: Polymorphic discriminator strategy drift detected across layers.
